topic: What music supervisors actually want — from 9 real briefs
cta: The supervisors who came back for Month 2 briefs came back because the first submission required zero follow-up.
caption_hook:
We've handled 9 briefs in 90 days.
Here's what every music supervisor actually wanted — from their words, not ours. ↓
caption_full:
We've handled 9 briefs in 90 days.
Here's what every music supervisor actually wanted — from their words, not ours. ↓

The supervisors who came back for Month 2 briefs came back →

#SyncMaster #AfricanComposers #SyncLicensing #MusicSupervisor

slides.eyebrow: Education · What music supervisors actually want — from 9 real briefs
slides.headline: We've handled 9 briefs in 90 days.
Here's what every music supervisor actually wanted — from their words, not ours.
slides.lede: ↓ · Here's what you need to know: — — — · WHAT THEY SAY THEY WANT

slides.headline:
"Rights-cleared tracks with stems."
"Quick turnaround."
"African sounds for
slides.lede:
diverse casting."
"Real African music, not fusion." · Here's what you need to know: Every brief. Every time. · — — — · WHAT THEY ACTUALLY MEAN

slides.headline: "Rights-cleared" means one-stop.
slides.lede:
One conversation. One deal. No separate master/sync clearance.
If you can't confirm one-stop, you're off the shortlist before the brief goes out.

slides.headline: "African sounds" means specific, not continental.
slides.lede:
Afrobeats is not the same as Highlife.
Amapiano is not the same as South African jazz.
Supervisors doing their homework know the difference. Assume they do. · Here's what you need to know: — — — · WHAT THEY NEVER SAY BUT ALWAYS NEED · Stems in the delivery.
Not "available on request." In the initial folder. · Complete metadata. If your track arrives without BPM, key, and mood tags, it adds friction.
Friction means a different track gets selected.

slides.headline: A clear fee position.
slides.lede:
"What's your rate?" answered before it's asked.
Supervisors who have to chase a fee quote move on. · Here's what you need to know: — — — · WHAT THEY REMEMBER · Music quality gets you the shortlist. Documentation quality gets you the placement.

slides.headline:
Three months of briefs confirmed this:
No rejected submission was rejected because of music quality.
slides.lede: Every rejection was documentation, rights, or turnaround. · Here's what you need to know: — — — · HOW TO SPEAK THEIR LANGUAGE

slides.headline: Submit — don't pitch.
slides.lede: One email: WAV master + stems + metadata card + rate card + rights confirmation. · Not five messages. Not "I'll send the stems tomorrow." · The supervisors who came back for Month 2 briefs came back because the first submission required zero follow-up.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
